perturbation.original_text: mPhase agrees to abide by the guidelines appearing on Schedule B of this Agreement specifying the dimensions, requirements and specifications, and the review process related to use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo. mPhase understands that these guidelines may be updated from time to time and agrees to abide by those updates as well as the guidelines provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: mPhase agrees to abide by the guidelines appearing on Schedule B of this Agreement specifying the dimensions, requirements and specifications, and the review process related to use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo. mPhase understands that these guidelines may be updated from time to time but is not obligated to abide by these updates unless mPhase provides explicit written consent to each update.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that mPhase agrees to abide by updates to the guidelines in Schedule B. The changed text contradicts this by stating that mPhase is not obligated to abide by updates unless they explicitly consent in writing. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding mPhase's obligations concerning updates to the co-branding guidelines. This affects compliance and enforcement since Lucent might assume mPhase is automatically in compliance with updated guidelines, while mPhase can claim they never explicitly consented.
perturbation.contradicted_text: mPhase agrees to abide by the guidelines appearing on Schedule B of this Agreement specifying the dimensions, requirements and specifications, and the review process related to use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo. mPhase understands that these guidelines may be updated from time to time but is not obligated to abide by these updates unless mPhase provides explicit written consent to each update.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that mPhase agrees to abide by updates to Schedule B guidelines. The changed text states that mPhase is NOT obligated to abide by these updates unless they explicitly consent in writing. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text in this case, contains this contradictory statement. This creates a conflict regarding mPhase's obligations concerning updates to the co-branding guidelines and aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed in multiple locations (in this case, implicitly within itself) with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: The Term of this Agreement will commence on the date above, and shall continue for a term of one (1) year. mPhase shall have the right to annually renew this agreement for a period of one year upon each annual expiration with the written consent of Lucent, which written consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. Either party wishing to terminate the Agreement must give written notice to the other party at least thirty (30) days prior to the desired date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: The Term of this Agreement will commence on the date above, and shall continue for a term of one (1) year. mPhase shall have the right to automatically renew this agreement for successive one year periods unless Lucent objects to renewal at least one day prior to the renewal date. Either party wishing to terminate the Agreement must give written notice to the other party at least thirty (30) days prior to the desired date of termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that mPhase has the right to renew the agreement with Lucent's written consent, which cannot be unreasonably withheld. The changed text states that the agreement automatically renews unless Lucent objects. This creates a contradiction: one version requires Lucent's active consent for renewal, while the other implies renewal is automatic unless Lucent takes action to prevent it. This affects renewal rights and notification obligations of both parties. This section 11 is contradicted by the new amendment, since it requires explicit consent from Lucent, while in the new change Lucent just needs to object.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Term of this Agreement will commence on the date above, and shall continue for a term of one (1) year. mPhase shall have the right to automatically renew this agreement for successive one year periods unless Lucent objects to renewal at least one day prior to the renewal date. Either party wishing to terminate the Agreement must give written notice to the other party at least thirty (30) days prior to the desired date of termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal process from requiring Lucent's explicit written consent to an automatic renewal unless Lucent actively objects. This directly contradicts the original requirement for Lucent's consent, creating a clear inconsistency in the renewal terms and obligations. The original text necessitates affirmative action from Lucent to renew, while the changed text only requires action from Lucent to prevent renewal. This incompatibility is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, mPhase shall immediately cease use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, provided, however, that mPhase shall have no obligation to remove such Co-Branding Logo from any Goods sold prior to the date of such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, mPhase shall immediately cease use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, and shall take all reasonable steps to remove the Co-Branding Logo from any Goods sold prior to the date of such termination, except where such removal is economically infeasible.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that upon termination, mPhase is not obligated to remove the co-branding logo from goods already sold. The changed text contradicts this by stating that mPhase shall take all reasonable steps to remove the logo from those same goods, unless it's economically infeasible. This creates a contradiction: one version explicitly states no obligation to remove, while the other requires reasonable steps to remove. This affects post-termination obligations and liability for continued use of the logo. Section 12 is being contradicted by this new amendment since the text is very clear about the goods sold, before the change was made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, mPhase shall immediately cease use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, and shall take all reasonable steps to remove the Co-Branding Logo from any Goods sold prior to the date of such termination, except where such removal is economically infeasible.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that mPhase has no obligation to remove the Co-Branding Logo from goods sold prior to termination. The changed text directly contradicts this by requiring mPhase to take reasonable steps to remove the logo from those same goods, unless it is economically infeasible. This creates a clear conflict in obligations, as one version says 'no obligation' while the other mandates 'reasonable steps,' leading to a strong in-text contradiction. The contradiction is in regards to obligations of removing logos from good sold before termination of the agreement.
